Hey, 
Lets pickup phase 4 from WEEK6_TODO

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- docs-consistency-guard: Documentation agent that audits docs for staleness and inconsistency AND generates/updates documentation on request. Use proactively when reviewing any PR that touches CLAUDE.md, docs/adr/, CONTRIBUTING.md, or docs/architecture.md. Also invoke when the user asks "is the documentation up to date?", "update the milestone", "scaffold the ADR for X", "add this to the ADR index", "add a TODO item", "scaffold a README for X", or "create a doc stub for Y." (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, Write, Edit)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- migration-safety: Specialized reviewer for Alembic migrations under backend/alembic/versions/. Audits for backward-incompatible operations that would break running code during a deploy, per working-agreement (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash)

- orchestration-abstraction-guard: Specialized reviewer that audits backend code for provider-specific branching that bypasses the OrchestrationProvider abstraction. Use this proactively when reviewing any PR that touches backend/app/orchestration/, backend/app/services/, or backend/app/api/ — especially any code path handling ADF or Airflow events, trigger bindings, or pipeline_runs. Also invoke when the user asks "does this go through the abstraction?" or similar. (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- commit-commands:clean_gone: Cleans up all git branches marked as [gone] (branches that have been deleted on the remote but still exist locally), including removing associated worktrees.
- commit-commands:commit-push-pr: Commit, push, and open a PR
- commit-commands:commit: Create a git commit
- claude-code-setup:claude-automation-recommender: Analyze a codebase and recommend Claude Code automations (hooks, subagents, skills, plugins, MCP servers). Use when user asks for automation recommendations, wants to optimize their Claude Code setup, mentions improving Claude Code workflows, asks how to first set up Claude Code for a project, or wants to know what Claude Code features they should use.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# Week 6 — Results dashboard + alerting · working TODO

> **Local scratch — gitignored** (`*_TODO.md`). Source of truth stays [docs/progress.md](docs/progress.md) + epic [#175](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/175). This file is the *execution plan*: ordering, file pointers, acceptance, commit boundaries. Update freely; don't commit.

**Exit gate:** Full results dashboard live across all source types; alerts firing with suppression.
**Scope:** 27 tasks (ADR [0022](docs/adr/0022-week6-prototype-adoption-and-chart-library.md)). Full 13-screen prototype set as dedicated pages — **Share is the only drawer; prototype wins on any drawer-vs-page / layout conflict.** Chart lib = **recharts** (lazy-loaded). Currently 2/27 (Results scaffold + severity colours, PR-C1).

**Working rules (CONTRIBUTING):** one functionality per commit · manually test before next · feature branch off `main` · conventional commits · `/code-review` agents before merge · run format/lint/typecheck/bandit/tests locally before push · fixup commits on open PRs (no amend+force) · file defects as issues (`Fixes #N`) with the W6 milestone.

**Prototype source:** project `317fec67-2b8a-498c-8f6b-523750916a8d` via `DesignSync` (`method=get_file path=templates/app/<Screen>.jsx`). `readme.md` = canonical spec. Build in real **antd** reading `theme.ts` — the `.jsx` is reference only.

---

git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
refactor(frontend): edit flows to dedicated pages

Replace the connection / suite / check edit drawers with deep-linkable
routed pages (ADR 0022 — prototype wins on drawer-vs-page; dedups #204):
- /connections/:id/edit  → ConnectionEdit (reuses ConnectionForm)
- /suites/:id/edit       → SuiteEdit (reuses SuiteForm)
- /suites/:id/checks/:id/edit → CheckEdit

Each edit page shares its create page's form component so the two surfaces
can't drift (ConnectionForm + SuiteForm gain an edit mode; the check form
helpers were already shared). Connections/Suites drop their drawer + editing
state for navigate(). Removed ConnectionDrawer, SuiteDrawer, CheckDrawer and
their tests; ported the edit-path assertions (type/env lock, target-removal
guard, config prefill, history button) to the new page tests. Added getCheck
for the deep-linkable check-edit page.

Version history stays a read-only drawer (alongside Share).

W6 Phase 4.2.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF

content.text: Now 4.3. Let me check `connectionVisuals` (avatars/icons) to build the richer source cards:

content.content:
1	import {
2	  CloudOutlined,
3	  DeploymentUnitOutlined,
4	  FolderOpenOutlined,
5	  InboxOutlined,
6	  NodeIndexOutlined,
7	  TableOutlined,
8	} from '@ant-design/icons';
9	import { Flex } from 'antd';
10	import type { ReactNode } from 'react';
11	
12	import type { ConnectionType } from '../../api/connections';
13	
14	/**
15	 * Per-datasource visual identity (icon + brand-ish accent) so the connection
16	 * list reads as recognisable products rather than a wall of identical cards.
17	 * Single source for the glyph + colour — the card avatar and anywhere else that
18	 * wants a type marker both read from here.
19	 */
20	const TYPE_VISUAL: Record<ConnectionType, { icon: ReactNode; color: string }> = {
21	  snowflake: { icon: <CloudOutlined />, color: '#29b5e8' },
22	  adls_gen2: { icon: <FolderOpenOutlined />, color: '#0078d4' },
23	  s3: { icon: <InboxOutlined />, color: '#ff9900' },
24	  unity_catalog: { icon: <TableOutlined />, color: '#ff3621' },
25	  adf: { icon: <DeploymentUnitOutlined />, color: '#0078d4' },
26	  airflow: { icon: <NodeIndexOutlined />, color: '#017cee' },
27	};
28	
29	/** A rounded-square icon avatar tinted with the datasource's accent colour. */
30	export function ConnectionTypeAvatar({ type, size = 40 }: { type: ConnectionType; size?: number }) {
31	  const { icon, color } = TYPE_VISUAL[type];
32	  return (
33	    <Flex
34	      align="center"
35	      justify="center"
36	      style={{
37	        width: size,
38	        height: size,
39	        flexShrink: 0,
40	        borderRadius: 10,
41	        // A soft tint of the accent (≈12% alpha) behind the full-strength glyph.
42	        background: `${color}1f`,
43	        color,
44	        fontSize: size * 0.5,
45	      }}
46	    >
47	      {icon}
48	    </Flex>
49	  );
50	}
51

content.content: Launching skill: code-review

content.text:
Review target: `main`

`high effort → 3+5 angles × 6 candidates → 1-vote verify (recall-biased) → ≤10 findings`

You are reviewing for **recall** at high effort: catch every real bug a careful
reviewer would catch in one sitting. At this level, catching real bugs matters
more than avoiding false positives. Err on the side of surfacing.

## Phase 0 — Gather the diff

Run `git diff @{upstream}...HEAD` (or `git diff main...HEAD` / `git diff HEAD~1`
if there's no upstream) to get the unified diff under review. If there are
uncommitted changes, or the range diff is empty, also run `git diff HEAD` and
include the working-tree changes in scope — the review often runs before the
commit. If a PR number, branch name, or file path was passed as an argument,
review that target instead. Treat this diff as the review scope.

## Phase 1 — Find candidates (3 correctness angles + 3 cleanup angles + 1 altitude angle + 1 conventions angle, up to 6 each)

Run **8 independent finder angles** via the Agent tool. Each
surfaces **up to 6 candidate findings** with `file`, `line`, a one-line
`summary`, and a concrete `failure_scenario`.

### Angle A — line-by-line diff scan

Read every hunk in the diff, line by line. Then Read the enclosing function for
each hunk — bugs in unchanged lines of a touched function are in scope (the PR
re-exposes or fails to fix them). For every line ask: what input, state, timing,
or platform makes this line wrong? Look for inverted/wrong conditions,
off-by-one, null/undefined deref, missing `await`, falsy-zero checks,
wrong-variable copy-paste, error swallowed in catch, unescaped regex metachars.

### Angle B — removed-behavior auditor

For every line the diff DELETES or replaces, name the invariant or behavior it
enforced, then search the new code for where that invariant is re-established.
If you can't find it, that's a candidate: a removed guard, a dropped error
path, a narrowed validation, a deleted test that was covering a real case.

### Angle C — cross-file tracer

For each function the diff changes, find its callers (Grep for the symbol) and
check whether the change breaks any call site: a new precondition, a changed
return shape, a new exception, a timing/ordering dependency. Also check callees:
does a parallel change in the same PR make a call unsafe?

### Reuse

The angles above hunt for bugs; this one and the next two hunt for cleanup in
the changed code. Flag new code that re-implements something the codebase
already has — Grep shared/utility modules and files adjacent to the change,
and name the existing helper to call instead.

### Simplification

Flag unnecessary complexity the diff adds: redundant or derivable state,
copy-paste with slight variation, deep nesting, dead code left behind. Name
the simpler form that does the same job.

### Efficiency

Flag wasted work the diff introduces: redundant computation or repeated I/O,
independent operations run sequentially, blocking work added to startup or
hot paths. Also flag long-lived objects built from closures or captured
environments — they keep the entire enclosing scope alive for the object's
lifetime (a memory leak when that scope holds large values); prefer a
class/struct that copies only the fields it needs. Name the cheaper
alternative.

### Altitude

Check that each change is implemented at the right depth, not as a fragile
bandaid. Special cases layered on shared infrastructure are a sign the fix
isn't deep enough — prefer generalizing the underlying mechanism over adding
special cases.

### Conventions (CLAUDE.md)

Find the CLAUDE.md files that govern the changed code: the user-level
~/.claude/CLAUDE.md, the repo-root CLAUDE.md, plus any CLAUDE.md or
CLAUDE.local.md in a directory that is an ancestor of a changed file (a
directory's CLAUDE.md only applies to files at or below it). Read each one
that exists, then check the diff for clear violations of the rules they state.

Only flag a violation when you can quote the exact rule and the exact line
that breaks it — no style preferences, no vague "spirit of the doc"
inferences. In the finding, name the CLAUDE.md path and quote the rule so the
report can cite it. If no CLAUDE.md applies, return nothing for this angle.

Cleanup, altitude, and conventions candidates use the same
`file`/`line`/`summary` shape; in `failure_scenario`, state the concrete
cost (what is duplicated, wasted, harder to maintain, or which CLAUDE.md rule
is broken) instead of a crash. Correctness bugs always outrank cleanup,
altitude, and conventions findings when the output cap forces a cut.

Pass every candidate with a nameable failure scenario through — finders that
silently drop half-believed candidates bypass the verify step and are the
dominant cause of misses.

## Phase 2 — Verify (1-vote, recall-biased)

Dedup near-duplicates (same defect, same location, same reason → keep one). For
each remaining candidate, run **one verifier** via the Agent tool:
give it the diff, the relevant file(s), and the candidate; it returns exactly
one of **CONFIRMED / PLAUSIBLE / REFUTED**.

**PLAUSIBLE by default** — do not refute a candidate for being "speculative" or
"depends on runtime state" when the state is realistic: concurrency races,
nil/undefined on a rare-but-reachable path (error handler, cold cache, missing
optional field), falsy-zero treated as missing, off-by-one on a boundary the
code does not exclude, retry storms / partial failures, regex/allowlist that
lost an anchor. These are PLAUSIBLE.

**REFUTED** only when constructible from the code: factually wrong (quote the
actual line); provably impossible (type/constant/invariant — show it); already
handled in this diff (cite the guard); or pure style with no observable effect.

Keep **CONFIRMED and PLAUSIBLE**. Drop REFUTED.

## Output

Return findings as a JSON array of at most 10 objects:

```json
[
  {
    "file": "path/to/file.ext",
    "line": 123,
    "summary": "one-sentence statement of the bug",
    "failure_scenario": "concrete inputs/state → wrong output/crash"
  }
]
```

Ranked most-severe first. If more than 10 survive, keep the 10 most
severe. If nothing survives verification, return `[]`.

content.text: I have the diff scope. Let me run the review finder angles in parallel via agents.

input.prompt:
You are a code-review finder for a React+TypeScript (antd, react-router v7) frontend. Review the branch diff `git diff main...HEAD` in /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/frontend. This is a refactor that replaces edit/create DRAWERS with routed PAGES for connections, suites, and checks, plus a categorized connection source-picker.

Run these THREE correctness angles and report candidate bugs (up to 6 total, the highest-confidence):

Angle A — line-by-line diff scan: Read every hunk, then Read the enclosing function. Look for inverted/wrong conditions, null/undefined deref, missing await, falsy-zero checks, wrong-variable copy-paste, swallowed errors, effect dependency bugs, react-router param/route ordering bugs.

Angle B — removed-behavior auditor: For each DELETED line (the three drawers: ConnectionDrawer.tsx, SuiteDrawer.tsx, CheckDrawer.tsx, and their tests), name the invariant/behavior it enforced and verify the new pages (ConnectionForm.tsx, SuiteForm.tsx, ConnectionNew.tsx, ConnectionEdit.tsx, SuiteNew.tsx, SuiteEdit.tsx, CheckEdit.tsx) re-establish it. Especially: form reset/prefill on remount, the "can't remove a target once set" guard, expectation-config rebuild, destroyOnHidden semantics, the CheckDrawer histScope reset, type/env immutability, secret omission on edit.

Angle C — cross-file tracer: For changed functions/new routes, check call sites (Grep). Verify route registration order in App.tsx (e.g. /suites/new vs /suites/:suiteId, /connections/:id/edit), navigate() targets match registered routes, getCheck/getConnection error handling, and that removed drawer imports aren't still referenced.

Key files to read: frontend/src/pages/{ConnectionNew,ConnectionEdit,SuiteNew,SuiteEdit,CheckEdit,Connections,Suites}.tsx, frontend/src/components/connections/{ConnectionForm,connectionSources}.tsx, frontend/src/components/suites/SuiteForm.tsx, frontend/src/App.tsx, frontend/src/api/suites.ts.

For each candidate output: file, line, one-line summary, and a concrete failure_scenario (inputs/state → wrong behavior). Only report candidates with a nameable, realistic failure scenario. Return a JSON array.

lastPrompt: Hey,  Lets pickup phase 4 from WEEK6_TODO

content.text:
Default htmlType is `'button'`, so the History and Cancel buttons won't submit the form. Good — no accidental-submit bug.

I have enough to finalize. Let me consolidate findings. The strongest concrete finding is the `useAsyncData` not refetching on route-param change across the three edit pages. Let me verify there's no `key` on the routes that would force remount, and confirm the failure path once more by checking if `Connections`/`Suites` list rows could ever navigate edit→edit. Actually, the most concrete reachable instance: a user on `/connections/A/edit` who uses browser **back then forward**, or has two connections and edits sequentially via... no. The cleanest reproducible case is deep-link + in-app `<Link>`-less. I'll report it scoped honestly.

Let me also double check: does `react-router` remount on param-only change? It does NOT by default. So stale data is real but only when the SAME edit route is navigated with a different param without an intermediate unmount. I'll include it as medium confidence.

Let me do one final scan of Angle A on `ConnectionForm` for the falsy-secret check: `secret: values.secret || undefined`. If a user legitimately entered a secret that's an empty string... empty string secret is meaningless, so `|| undefined` is correct. No falsy-zero issue (secrets aren't numeric).

Final answer:

Based on my three-angle review, here are the candidate bugs (highest-confidence). I verified and discarded several false leads (the antd `Alert title` prop is correct for antd v6; route ordering is fine under react-router v7 specificity ranking; the "can't remove target once set" guard and secret-omission-on-edit are correctly preserved; no stale drawer imports remain).

```json
[
  {
    "file": "frontend/src/pages/CheckEdit.tsx, ConnectionEdit.tsx, SuiteEdit.tsx",
    "line": "CheckEdit ~35; ConnectionEdit ~16; SuiteEdit ~18 (the useAsyncData call)",
    "summary": "Edit pages load data via useAsyncData, whose effect depends only on its internal nonce — not on the route params — so navigating between two sibling edit URLs without an intervening unmount keeps showing the FIRST entity's data and form.",
    "failure_scenario": "User is on /connections/A/edit, then changes the URL (address bar, a deep link, or browser forward after a back) to /connections/B/edit. React Router reuses the same <ConnectionEdit/> element (no key, params-only change → no remount). useAsyncData's effect ([nonce] deps) does not re-run, so getConnection still holds A's data; the form stays prefilled with A and a Save would PATCH connection B with A's name/config. Same shape for SuiteEdit and CheckEdit (CheckEdit's seed effect is keyed on [check] which never updates, and the history drawer / dry-run reset all assume a fresh mount).",
    "confidence": "medium"
  },
  {
    "file": "frontend/src/pages/CheckEdit.tsx",
    "line": "~96-105 (seed useEffect) and ~118 (no resetFields)",
    "summary": "CheckEdit's seed effect calls form.setFieldsValue without a preceding form.resetFields(); combined with the non-remounting useAsyncData above, a switch to a check using a different expectation_type can leave stale config kwargs from the prior check in the form store.",
    "failure_scenario": "The deleted CheckDrawer deliberately did form.resetFields() BEFORE prefilling 'because antd keeps values for unmounted fields, so stale config can survive an open→close→open with a different check.' CheckEdit dropped that reset (relying on fresh mount). If CheckEdit is re-seeded for a different check without remounting (see finding 1: param-only navigation), antd retains the previous expectation's config.* fields that the new expectation doesn't declare; buildCheckPayload rebuilds only the selected expectation's fields, but any overlapping field name (e.g. config.column) carries the old value into the saved check.",
    "confidence": "low"
  },
  {
    "file": "frontend/src/pages/Connections.tsx",
    "line": "~104 (onEdit navigate) — vs deleted onSaved clearHealth",
    "summary": "Behavioral change (not a crash): the deleted ConnectionDrawer.onSaved called clearHealth(editing.id) to invalidate a prior connectivity test after a host/credential edit; moving edit to a separate page removed that call.",
    "failure_scenario": "In the old flow editing a connection's host then saving cleared its cached health badge so a stale 'healthy' result wasn't shown against the new host. Now editing navigates to /connections/:id/edit (unmounting Connections and dropping the in-memory health map entirely), so on return health is empty and re-derived — the invariant is incidentally preserved by the unmount. Only a concern if Connections is ever kept mounted (e.g. converted to a nested/outlet layout); flagging because the explicit invalidation intent was silently dropped, not re-established.",
    "confidence": "low"
  }
]
```

Summary of what I checked and cleared (to save the caller time):
- Angle A: ConnectionForm's reset-on-`type`-change effect correctly clears prior-type fields; `secret: values.secret || undefined` correctly omits the secret on edit (no field rendered); History/Cancel buttons are antd default `htmlType="button"` so they don't accidentally submit; SuiteForm's "can't remove a target once set" guard is intact.
- Angle B: type/env immutability preserved (`ConnectionForm` shows them read-only via `isEdit`), secret omission preserved (`showSecret={!isEdit}`), suite `connection_id` locked on edit, expectation-config rebuild via `buildCheckPayload` unchanged. The `histScope` history-drawer reset and DryRunPreview in-place reset are not re-added but are naturally satisfied by per-route remount (only at risk under finding 1's no-remount path).
- Angle C: no stale `ConnectionDrawer`/`SuiteDrawer`/`CheckDrawer` imports remain; `getCheck`/`getSuite`/`getConnection` all exist and error-handle (`getConnection` is best-effort `.catch(() => null)`); navigate() targets all match registered routes; route registration is correct (react-router v7 ranks by specificity, so `/suites/new` vs `/suites/:suiteId` ordering is safe); `connectionSources` covers all 6 types via a total `Record<ConnectionType,...>` so no source is silently dropped.
- False positive avoided: the four new pages use `<Alert title=... />` — this is the CURRENT prop in antd v6 (`message` is the deprecated alias), so it is correct, not a bug.

The highest-value finding is #1 (shared root cause across all three edit pages); #2 is a downstream consequence of it.

input.prompt:
You are a code-review finder for a React+TypeScript (antd) frontend. Review the branch diff `git diff main...HEAD` in /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/frontend. This refactor replaces edit/create DRAWERS with routed PAGES (connections, suites, checks) and adds a categorized connection source-picker.

Run these angles and report up to 6 highest-value candidates total:

As written the critical path is max(getSuite,getCheck)+getConnection; optimal is max(getSuite+getConnection, getCheck).",
    "failure_scenario": "On a check whose suite has many checks (getCheck slow) the page still pays a full extra serial getConnection round-trip after both resolve — an avoidable added latency on every check-edit open, the most frequent authoring action. Restructure: const suiteP=getSuite(); const [check, suite]=await Promise.all([getCheck(...), suiteP]); const connection=await suiteP.then(s=>getConnection(s.connection_id)).catch(()=>null)."

    "line": "11-26",
    "summary": "connectionSources.ts introduces a SECOND finer-grained type grouping (SOURCE_CATEGORY: Orchestration/Warehouses/Lakehouses/Cloud Storage) alongside the existing CONNECTION_KIND (datasource/orchestration) and DATASOURCE_CATEGORY (snowflake/flatfile/unity_catalog) in api/connections.ts. The header comment justifies it as picker-only presentation, which is legitimate — but it's now a third per-type map that must stay in lockstep when a 7th connection type is added.",
    "failure_scenario": "Adding a connection type (the codebase explicitly anticipates dbt-as-OrchestrationProvider, ADR 0011) requires editing CONNECTION_KIND, DATASOURCE_CATEGORY, AND SOURCE_CATEGORY (plus CONNECTION_BLURB). SOURCE_CATEGORY is a non-Partial Record<ConnectionType,...> so TS will catch a missing key — but CONNECTION_BLURB being a full Record too means two more required maps; easy to add the type to the API maps and forget the picker maps, and the new type silently vanishes from the add-connection grid (sourcesByCategory filters empty categories, so no error — it just won't be addable)."
  },
  {
    "angle": "CONVENTIONS (orchestration-as-datasource guard)",
    "file": "frontend/src/components/suites/SuiteForm.tsx, frontend/src/components/connections/connectionSources.ts",
    "line": "SuiteForm.tsx:53; suiteTarget.ts:13-24",

SuiteForm filters the connection picker to CONNECTION_KIND==='datasource' (line 53), and targetKind() returns null for adf/airflow (suiteTarget.ts:23), so orchestration providers cannot back a suite. connectionSources.ts leads with Orchestration only for the add-CONNECTION picker (creating the connection row is correct per CLAUDE.md §4 — ADF/Airflow are connections rows), never the suite picker. CLAUDE.md §11: 'Don't add ADF or Airflow as a queryable datasource in the connection editor / check editor / suite model' — not violated.",

content.text:
Both passes are in. Consolidated verified findings:

1. **Correctness (real, low-reachability):** the three edit pages fetch via `useAsyncData`, whose effect keys on an internal nonce — not the route param. Navigating `/connections/A/edit` → `/connections/B/edit` param-only (no unmount) keeps A's data and would PATCH B with A's values. Same root cause drops CheckEdit's old `resetFields`-before-seed safety.
2. **Efficiency (minor):** CheckEdit awaits `getConnection` sequentially after the `Promise.all`.
3. **Altitude (cleanup):** the 3 edit pages duplicate the load+header+Card shell.

I'll fix the correctness issue (#1) with a keyed remount and the cheap fetch fix (#2). The shell duplication (#3) I'll leave as a noted follow-up rather than expand scope mid-phase. Let me apply the fixes:

23:| **Current week** | **Week 6 of 8 (Results dashboard + alerting) — IN PROGRESS (13/27)**. Weeks 1–5 complete. Dashboard + run-detail track done (PR #333 + PR-C1); results filters + orchestration polish in review (PR-C2). Remaining: alerting (6) + prototype Phases 4–6 |
26:| **Week-5 exit gate** | Async runs with live progress across all datasource types; scheduling operational. — **MET**: run paths across all datasource types done (Snowflake + flat-file batch + UC), run **progress** + **cancel** APIs shipped + the **live run-progress UI (A3)** consuming them, `error`/`skip` operational statuses landed, orchestration `/pipelines` + gap recovery + **scheduling backend (A7)** + the **scheduled-runs UI (A6)** in (scheduling operational end-to-end), **run-history retention sweep (A8)** done, **run-now panel** shipped. The one roadmap Execution-UI task not built — the **recent-runs audit table** — was deliberately **moved to Week 6** to be built once in its prototype page-home (`/dashboard`), not a gate item. Weeks 1–4 exit gates also met — see [CLAUDE.md §13](../CLAUDE.md). Live warehouse/file run remains the deferred Week-1 smoke. |
27:| **Next milestone** | **Finish Week 6 — Results dashboard + alerting** (13/27 done). Shipped: the dashboard widgets (health cards, 7-day trend, per-suite bars, recent-runs table, per-check trend), the routed run-detail page + CSV/JSON export, nav/shell restructure, and the results filter bar + orchestration polish (PR-C2 in review). **Remaining:** the `ResultPublisher` alerting seam + Teams notifications (6 tasks) and prototype Phases 4–6 — New-Suite/edit pages, New-Connection source-select split, Profile content, Settings, Admin reconcile, error pages (ADR [0022](adr/0022-week6-prototype-adoption-and-chart-library.md), recharts decided). |
28:| **Open issues** | **41 open** — roadmap *features* are tracked as ⬜ checkboxes per week (below), not GitHub issues, so these are follow-ups / polish only. **Week-1 carryover (→ W7):** [#168](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/168) (silent token refresh) / [#169](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/169) (real Key Vault) / [#170](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/170) (prod-docs gate). **Backend:** [#124](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/124) (DQ dimensions) / [#194](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/194) + [#195](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/195) (Snowflake key-pair: encrypted keys / GX deprecated path) / [#92](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/92) (ADF webhook URL) / [#327](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/327) (profiler N+1 batching — split from #147). **Frontend (W4):** [#192](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/192) (nav prefix-match) / [#197](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/197) (Select test helper) / [#199](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/199) (toast helper) / [#204](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/204) (drawer/delete dedup) / [#205](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/205) (catalog↔GX contract test) / [#128](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/128) (full-stack E2E/Playwright). **Governance polish:** #8/#10/#17/#18/#19/#20. **Closed in Week 5:** #122 (skip/error), #129, #146, #147 (PR #329), #171, #172, #201, #212, #215, #216, #227, #303, **#317 (PR #328)** + the Week-5 epic #174. |
29:| **Open PRs** | **PR-C2** — results filters (env/datasource/suite/date) + orchestration 30s poll & DQ-run correlation (Week-6 Phase 3) |
178:- [x] ✅ Add connection drawer — type-specific form fields per connection type — spec-driven `ConnectionTypeFields` from `CONNECTION_FORM_SPECS` (all six types; Snowflake password/key-pair auth toggle) — [PR #196](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/196) (+ Snowflake key-pair backend [PR #193](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/193))
180:- [x] ✅ Connection re-auth UI — surface expired tokens, inline refresh action — `ReauthModal` (single-secret rotate+verify) + edit drawer + delete via the card actions menu — [PR #198](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/198)
181:- [x] ✅ ADLS/S3 connection form — account URL, SAS toggle — covered by the spec-driven drawer ([PR #196](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/196)) _(container browser + managed-identity/IAM-role modes deferred with the backend, ADR 0010/0011)_
182:- [x] ✅ Databricks connection form — workspace URL, PAT, warehouse id — covered by the spec-driven drawer ([PR #196](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/196)) _(live SQL-Warehouse picker deferred; warehouse id is a text field)_
187:- [x] ✅ Flat file check editor — file path + format selector — the datasource-shaped **run-target editor** on the suite drawer ([PR #246](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/246)) authors a flat-file target (`path` + optional CSV/Parquet `file_format`), so ADLS/S3 suites become runnable; the generic catalog editor ([PR #203](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/203)) already covers flat-file expectations. _(Container/regex **batch-resolution** inputs remain a post-v1 polish — needs the flat-file listing surface.)_
189:- [x] ✅ **Monaco SQL editor for custom check** — a "Custom SQL" category authoring a GX `UnexpectedRowsExpectation` (ADR 0019): a lazy-loaded, locally-bundled Monaco editor (`SqlEditorField`, its own `monaco` Vite chunk so the eager bundle is untouched) + a client-side mirror of the backend read-only/single-statement guardrail (`customSql.ts`) for inline feedback. Gated to SQL datasources (Snowflake / Unity Catalog) in both the create page and the edit drawer via `expectationsByCategoryFor(connectionType)`. _(backend unblocked by ADR 0019 / [PR #258](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/258); Snowflake keyword autocomplete deferred — Monaco ships SQL syntax highlighting only)_
190:- [x] ✅ Column profiler panel — inline in check editor, loads on table / file selection — `ColumnProfilePanel` (shared by the create page `CheckNew` + the edit drawer `CheckDrawer`): a collapsed-by-default panel that profiles one column of the suite's run target (#215) via `POST /suites/{id}/profile` (no persistence) and renders null count/fraction, distinct count, min/max, and top values — so the author can ground a range / allowed-set / null threshold in real data before saving. The profiled column **pre-fills from the check's `column` config field** (render-phase prop-sync, like `DryRunPreview`) but stays editable to explore neighbouring columns; `table`/`schema`/`catalog` (SQL) or `path`/`file_format` (flat file) are pulled from the suite target. Disabled with a reason until the suite has a table/file target + a column is entered; backend limits (no credential / unreachable / unsupported type) surface as a clean error alert. 4 vitest (disabled-states / pre-filled-profile with request-shape assertion / error). _(PR-D2; `ColumnProfilePanel.tsx` + `api/suites.ts` `profileColumns`)_
191:- [x] ✅ **Check dry-run button** — preview pass/fail inline before saving — `DryRunPreview` component (shared by the create page `CheckNew` + the edit drawer `CheckDrawer`): runs the in-progress check against the suite's live target via `POST /suites/{id}/checks/dryrun` and renders the severity outcome (status tag + `metric_value` + observed/expected), **no persistence**. Reuses `buildCheckPayload` (so the preview runs exactly the saved check's config/thresholds) + the severity colour map; `table`/`schema` come from the suite's run target (#215, fetched via new `getSuite`). Disabled with a reason until an expectation is picked + the suite has a table target; backend limits (Snowflake-only, no-credential/unreachable) surface as a clean error alert (dev fail-softs to a 502 with fake creds). 4 vitest (disabled-states / success-outcome with request-shape assertion / error) + an e2e button-enabled assertion. _(PR-D1; `DryRunPreview.tsx` + `api/suites.ts` `getSuite`/`dryRunCheck`)_
192:- [x] ✅ **Check version history drawer — see previous config before overwriting** — a `check_versions` table snapshots a check's editable state (name/expectation/config/thresholds + author) on create, on every *real* update (no-op PATCHes skipped via `session.is_modified`), and per imported check; `GET /suites/{id}/checks/{cid}/versions` (view-gated, newest-first, author resolved server-side). The **History** button on `CheckDrawer` opens `CheckHistoryDrawer` — newest-first list, latest tagged **Current**, each row showing expectation label + pretty-printed config + `Warn ≥/Fail ≥/Critical ≥` thresholds + author/timestamp. View-only; **restore deferred to [#283](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/283)**. Backend migration verified up/down; 7 backend + 6 frontend tests. _(#280; backend [PR #281](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/281) + frontend [PR #282](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/282); ADR 0019 custom-SQL configs stay readable via pretty-print)_
196:- [x] ✅ Suite sharing panel — add / remove users, assign roles inline — **Share** button on the suite detail opens `SharePanel` (drawer): lists collaborators by name (from the enriched `ShareRead`) and, for owner/admin, adds/changes/removes them. Add picker is a debounced `GET /users/search` type-ahead filtering out the owner + already-shared users; permission is the view/edit/admin ladder. Gated on **`SuiteRead.my_permission`** — a new field stamping the caller's effective level (owner/admin/edit/view), populated on create/get/update + batched across the list via `suite_authz.effective_permissions` (one shares `IN`-query, no N+1) — so a non-owner admin gets the manage controls and a viewer a read-only list. Backend enablers landed first: **PR-E2** (`GET /users/search` behind a FastAPI-free `user_service` — case-insensitive email/display_name substring, 2-char min, limit≤50, LIKE-escaped — + `ShareRead` enriched with `email`/`display_name` via a joined read-only `Share.user`). 6 component tests (list-by-name, empty, search→grant with owner/existing filtered, change, revoke, read-only non-admin) + backend `my_permission` + `/users/search` tests. _(PR-E2 + PR-E3)_
198:- [x] ✅ Suite export / import UI (download JSON, upload JSON) — **Export** button on the suite detail header downloads `<suite>.json` from `GET /suites/{id}/export` via a transient-anchor blob (`utils/download.ts` — `downloadJson` + `toFilenameStem`); **Import** button on the suites header opens `ImportSuiteDrawer` — drag/drop a `.json`, client-side parse + shape-check (fail-fast on non-suite/malformed JSON), pick a target connection, `POST /suites/import`. The parsed document is handed back **untouched** so thresholds round-trip exactly (string-or-number Decimal encoding never coerced). 11 tests (download lib: stem slug + blob/anchor/revoke; drawer: valid→import-with-faithful-document, non-suite/malformed-JSON rejection, connection-gated Import). _(PR-E1; backend ready from Week 3)_
201:> Model the UI on [GX Cloud](https://greatexpectations.io/gx-cloud/): dedicated **full-page create flows** that **classify** what you're creating, keeping the lighter drawers for *edit*. Plan: `~/.claude/plans/fancy-moseying-raccoon.md`. Decided: in-app results page (Grafana deferred to an optional post-v1 ops add-on, never the per-suite authz path — ADR 0018 pending); full-page create + drawer edit; connections/checks redesign now (backends ready), results after the Week-5 run-enablement backend.
202:- [x] ✅ **Connections — dedicated, classified add page** — `/connections/new` splits the type picker into **Data sources** vs **Orchestration** sections (CLAUDE.md §4), then the spec-driven form; the list page is grouped the same way. `CONNECTION_KIND` is the single source for the split. Edit/re-auth/delete stay on the drawer. _(PR-A1; `ConnectionNew.tsx` + sectioned `Connections.tsx`)_
203:- [x] ✅ **Connections — `ConnectionDrawer` trimmed to edit-only** — create now lives on the page; the drawer drops the type/env Selects + secret + create branch, shows type/env read-only, and the list owns a simpler `editing: Connection | null` state. _(PR-A2)_
207:**Week 4 total: 26 / 26 ✅ — complete** _(connection manager UI complete (6/6); check editor core shipped + **dry-run preview** (PR-D1) + **column profiler panel** (PR-D2) + the datasource-shaped **run-target editor** (PR #246 — closes the flat-file + Unity Catalog target inputs, making suites runnable); **check version-history drawer** (#280; backend #281 + frontend #282 — view-only, restore deferred to #283); **suite export/import UI** (PR-E1); **suite sharing panel** (PR-E2 backend + PR-E3 UI, gated on `SuiteRead.my_permission`); **GX-Cloud redesign complete (4/4)** — classified connection + check create pages, edit-only drawers, suite-route/categorized picker (PR-A1/A2/B1/B2); PR-3c polish 3/3 (Settings `extra="forbid"` shipped via the `.env`/`.env.app` split, #209); **Monaco custom-SQL editor** (ADR 0019 — custom-SQL as a GX `UnexpectedRowsExpectation`; backend #258 + frontend #259); **admin control centre** (#289 — workspace-admin authz + `/admin/{suites,users,access}` + the `/admin` page & 403, pulling the Week-7 prototype-adoption admin tasks forward))_
238:> The roadmap's 5th Execution-UI task — **recent-runs audit table** — was **moved to Week 6** (counted there). Why: the [DataQ Design System](#week-6--results-dashboard--alerting) prototype makes the **Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard** (`/dashboard`, the [Week-6 task](#week-6--results-dashboard--alerting)) the page-home for the recent-runs widget, and its row drill-down target is the Week-6 routed run-detail page (`/results/:runId`, [#268](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/268) — promotes `Results.tsx`'s in-page `openRun` state to a route). Building a standalone table on `/results` now and rebuilding it on `/dashboard` in W6 is throwaway work; the dashboard also gates on the chart-library decision first. Deferred to build it **once, in its final home** — see Week 6.
239:- [x] ✅ Run now panel — suite picker, env / datasource, notification target — `RunNowPanel` (`components/runs/`): a **cross-suite** run launcher (modal off a **Run now** button in the Results header), distinct from the suite-detail **Run** button (#245) which runs one suite in context. A searchable suite `Select` filtered to the `owner`/`admin`/`edit` ladder the backend `POST /suites/{id}/run` enforces (view-only suites never appear); a read-only `Descriptions` readout of the picked suite's **env** (tag) + **datasource** (connection name + type) + **target** (one-line `catalog.schema.table` / flat-file `path`, mirroring `suiteTarget.ts`). A targetless suite blocks **Run** with an inline reason rather than a backend 422. On trigger it hands off to the shared `LiveRunProgress` drawer (modal closes; run watched + cancellable). **Notification target ships as a clearly-labelled disabled placeholder** ("coming in Week 6") — no notification backend exists yet (Teams/`ResultPublisher` alert routing is Week 6), so it doesn't jump the gun. Self-contained (owns its data fetch + progress drawer) so the Week-6 UI rework can drop it onto the dedicated Execution page unchanged. 3 component tests (no-runnable empty state, env/datasource/target render + trigger, targetless-suite block).
240:- [x] ✅ Live run progress UI — check-by-check status with spinner + cancel button — `LiveRunProgress` drawer (`components/runs/`): opens on the run id returned by the manual **Run** button (#245) instead of bouncing to `/results`, and polls `GET /runs/{id}/progress` (#301) on a self-scheduling timeout (not `setInterval` — no overlapping fetches) until the run is terminal (`succeeded`/`failed`/`cancelled`), then stops. Shows the run-status tag + a completed/total `Progress` bar + a per-check list (a `Spin`+"pending" while a check's `status` is null, its severity `Tag` once resolved). An editor (`canRun`) gets a **Cancel** button (`POST /runs/{id}/cancel` #302) on a non-terminal run; cancel reflects the terminal state immediately + the next poll confirms it. On terminal, a "View full results →" link to `/results`. Body keyed by run id so opening a new run restarts polling; `pollMs` is injectable for deterministic tests. 7 component tests (pending/resolved render, poll-until-terminal-then-stop, cancel happy path, cancel hidden when terminal / non-editor, fetch-error alert). **(A3)**
242:- [ ] 🔵 Recent runs audit table with drill-down link to results — **moved to Week 6** (counted there), folded into the Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard (`/dashboard`) where the prototype homes the recent-runs widget. Rationale in the group note above + the Week-6 task.

The **manual Run button** (PR #245) + the **run-target editor** (PR #246) also shipped earlier this week. **Closeout cleanup:** profiler `null_fraction` DRY ([#147](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/147), PR #329) + `RUN_BAR_STATUS` refactor ([#317](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/317), PR #328); profiler N+1 perf split to backlog [#327](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/327). **Moved to Week 6:** recent-runs audit table — see the Execution-UI note above + the Week-6 task.)_
249:## Week 6 — Results dashboard + alerting
255:- [x] ✅ **Results page scaffold** (PR-C1) — the in-app `/results` page + sidebar nav (Connections · Suites · **Results** · Profile). **Runs tab**: a runs table (suite name, status, triggered-by, started, duration) with a status filter, each row opening a **run-detail drawer** that drills into the per-check results (check name + expectation, severity tag, `metric_value`, observed value). **Pipeline runs tab**: the orchestration monitoring feed (`pipeline_runs`) with a provider filter. Pure presentation helpers (`resultsFormat.ts` — duration/timestamp formatters, status→colour maps) are unit-tested; `api/runs.ts` mirrors the C0b schemas. Demo seed (`demo_data.py`) now lands runs + results (pass/pass/warn/fail spread) + two pipeline-runs + suite targets so the page has real content. 10 vitest + 2 Playwright (`results.spec.ts`). _The rich widgets below build on this scaffold._
277:> Adopt the **DataQ Design System** prototype (claude.ai/design project *"DataQ Design System"*, reverse-engineered from the shipped indigo/antd app — its `readme.md` is the spec) — **the full 13-screen set this week**, not a subset (ADR [0022](adr/0022-week6-prototype-adoption-and-chart-library.md); the earlier 6-task scope under-counted it — Profile content, **Settings** (pulled in from Week 7), the Admin layout-reconcile, and the New-Connection source-select split were missing). The **foundation already matches** the product (`theme.ts` palette, `resultsFormat.ts` status model, the 56px-header/220px-sider shell), so this is **not a re-skin** — it adopts the prototype's **structure**: every create/edit flow becomes a dedicated, deep-linkable **page** (the **Share** drawer stays the *only* drawer), login lands on an **Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard**, and standard **error pages** ship. **Where the prototype differs from the shipped app (drawer-vs-page, layout), the prototype wins.** Components are reproduced with **real antd** — the prototype's framework-free recreations + `_ds_bundle.js` are reference only (per the design README). Post-v1 items (marketing, dark mode) + the polish passes (KPI honesty, visual-fidelity) stay in Week 7 / Post-v1 below. The intentionally-dropped prototype bits (the **"View as" role switch**, the **component bundle**, the **teal palette**) are recorded in Post-v1 as *not adopted*.
282:- [ ] ⬜ **New Suite → dedicated page** (`/suites/new`, `SuiteFlow`) — extract the `SuiteDrawer` **create** path into a routed *details / source* page reusing `suiteTarget.ts` + the run-target editor (PR #246). Removes the drawer's create branch; `Suites.tsx` swaps `setDrawer({open:true})` for `navigate('/suites/new')`. _Design ref: `templates/app/SuiteFlow.jsx` (New Suite → Details/Source) → `CheckFlow.jsx` (Define Rules); readme §"Templates" full-page-flows note._
283:- [ ] ⬜ **Edit flows → dedicated pages** — unify edit onto the existing create pages via an `:id` route + prefill, reusing the already-extracted form specs (`connectionFormSpec.ts` / `checkForm.ts` / `suiteTarget.ts`): connection edit `/connections/:id/edit` → `ConnectionNew`; suite edit `/suites/:id/edit` → `SuiteFlow`; check edit `/suites/:id/checks/:checkId/edit` → `CheckNew`. **Removes** the `ConnectionDrawer` / `CheckDrawer` / `SuiteDrawer` **edit** drawers; the parent pages drop their `editing` / `drawer` state for `navigate()`. (Dedups with [#204](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/204).) _Design ref: `templates/app/ConnectionFlow.jsx` (Configuration), `SuiteFlow.jsx`, `CheckFlow.jsx` — the create flows; edit reuses the same page shape, prefilled._

180:- [x] ✅ Connection re-auth UI — surface expired tokens, inline refresh action — `ReauthModal` (single-secret rotate+verify) + edit drawer + delete via the card actions menu — [PR #198](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/198)
181:- [x] ✅ ADLS/S3 connection form — account URL, SAS toggle — covered by the spec-driven drawer ([PR #196](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/196)) _(container browser + managed-identity/IAM-role modes deferred with the backend, ADR 0010/0011)_
182:- [x] ✅ Databricks connection form — workspace URL, PAT, warehouse id — covered by the spec-driven drawer ([PR #196](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/196)) _(live SQL-Warehouse picker deferred; warehouse id is a text field)_
187:- [x] ✅ Flat file check editor — file path + format selector — the datasource-shaped **run-target editor** on the suite drawer ([PR #246](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/246)) authors a flat-file target (`path` + optional CSV/Parquet `file_format`), so ADLS/S3 suites become runnable; the generic catalog editor ([PR #203](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/203)) already covers flat-file expectations. _(Container/regex **batch-resolution** inputs remain a post-v1 polish — needs the flat-file listing surface.)_
189:- [x] ✅ **Monaco SQL editor for custom check** — a "Custom SQL" category authoring a GX `UnexpectedRowsExpectation` (ADR 0019): a lazy-loaded, locally-bundled Monaco editor (`SqlEditorField`, its own `monaco` Vite chunk so the eager bundle is untouched) + a client-side mirror of the backend read-only/single-statement guardrail (`customSql.ts`) for inline feedback. Gated to SQL datasources (Snowflake / Unity Catalog) in both the create page and the edit drawer via `expectationsByCategoryFor(connectionType)`. _(backend unblocked by ADR 0019 / [PR #258](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/258); Snowflake keyword autocomplete deferred — Monaco ships SQL syntax highlighting only)_
190:- [x] ✅ Column profiler panel — inline in check editor, loads on table / file selection — `ColumnProfilePanel` (shared by the create page `CheckNew` + the edit drawer `CheckDrawer`): a collapsed-by-default panel that profiles one column of the suite's run target (#215) via `POST /suites/{id}/profile` (no persistence) and renders null count/fraction, distinct count, min/max, and top values — so the author can ground a range / allowed-set / null threshold in real data before saving. The profiled column **pre-fills from the check's `column` config field** (render-phase prop-sync, like `DryRunPreview`) but stays editable to explore neighbouring columns; `table`/`schema`/`catalog` (SQL) or `path`/`file_format` (flat file) are pulled from the suite target. Disabled with a reason until the suite has a table/file target + a column is entered; backend limits (no credential / unreachable / unsupported type) surface as a clean error alert. 4 vitest (disabled-states / pre-filled-profile with request-shape assertion / error). _(PR-D2; `ColumnProfilePanel.tsx` + `api/suites.ts` `profileColumns`)_
191:- [x] ✅ **Check dry-run button** — preview pass/fail inline before saving — `DryRunPreview` component (shared by the create page `CheckNew` + the edit drawer `CheckDrawer`): runs the in-progress check against the suite's live target via `POST /suites/{id}/checks/dryrun` and renders the severity outcome (status tag + `metric_value` + observed/expected), **no persistence**. Reuses `buildCheckPayload` (so the preview runs exactly the saved check's config/thresholds) + the severity colour map; `table`/`schema` come from the suite's run target (#215, fetched via new `getSuite`). Disabled with a reason until an expectation is picked + the suite has a table target; backend limits (Snowflake-only, no-credential/unreachable) surface as a clean error alert (dev fail-softs to a 502 with fake creds). 4 vitest (disabled-states / success-outcome with request-shape assertion / error) + an e2e button-enabled assertion. _(PR-D1; `DryRunPreview.tsx` + `api/suites.ts` `getSuite`/`dryRunCheck`)_
192:- [x] ✅ **Check version history drawer — see previous config before overwriting** — a `check_versions` table snapshots a check's editable state (name/expectation/config/thresholds + author) on create, on every *real* update (no-op PATCHes skipped via `session.is_modified`), and per imported check; `GET /suites/{id}/checks/{cid}/versions` (view-gated, newest-first, author resolved server-side). The **History** button on `CheckDrawer` opens `CheckHistoryDrawer` — newest-first list, latest tagged **Current**, each row showing expectation label + pretty-printed config + `Warn ≥/Fail ≥/Critical ≥` thresholds + author/timestamp. View-only; **restore deferred to [#283](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/283)**. Backend migration verified up/down; 7 backend + 6 frontend tests. _(#280; backend [PR #281](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/281) + frontend [PR #282](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/282); ADR 0019 custom-SQL configs stay readable via pretty-print)_
196:- [x] ✅ Suite sharing panel — add / remove users, assign roles inline — **Share** button on the suite detail opens `SharePanel` (drawer): lists collaborators by name (from the enriched `ShareRead`) and, for owner/admin, adds/changes/removes them. Add picker is a debounced `GET /users/search` type-ahead filtering out the owner + already-shared users; permission is the view/edit/admin ladder. Gated on **`SuiteRead.my_permission`** — a new field stamping the caller's effective level (owner/admin/edit/view), populated on create/get/update + batched across the list via `suite_authz.effective_permissions` (one shares `IN`-query, no N+1) — so a non-owner admin gets the manage controls and a viewer a read-only list. Backend enablers landed first: **PR-E2** (`GET /users/search` behind a FastAPI-free `user_service` — case-insensitive email/display_name substring, 2-char min, limit≤50, LIKE-escaped — + `ShareRead` enriched with `email`/`display_name` via a joined read-only `Share.user`). 6 component tests (list-by-name, empty, search→grant with owner/existing filtered, change, revoke, read-only non-admin) + backend `my_permission` + `/users/search` tests. _(PR-E2 + PR-E3)_
198:- [x] ✅ Suite export / import UI (download JSON, upload JSON) — **Export** button on the suite detail header downloads `<suite>.json` from `GET /suites/{id}/export` via a transient-anchor blob (`utils/download.ts` — `downloadJson` + `toFilenameStem`); **Import** button on the suites header opens `ImportSuiteDrawer` — drag/drop a `.json`, client-side parse + shape-check (fail-fast on non-suite/malformed JSON), pick a target connection, `POST /suites/import`. The parsed document is handed back **untouched** so thresholds round-trip exactly (string-or-number Decimal encoding never coerced). 11 tests (download lib: stem slug + blob/anchor/revoke; drawer: valid→import-with-faithful-document, non-suite/malformed-JSON rejection, connection-gated Import). _(PR-E1; backend ready from Week 3)_

The **foundation already matches** the product (`theme.ts` palette, `resultsFormat.ts` status model, the 56px-header/220px-sider shell), so this is **not a re-skin** — it adopts the prototype's **structure**: every create/edit flow becomes a dedicated, deep-linkable **page** (the **Share** drawer stays the *only* drawer), login lands on an **Enhanced Monitoring Dashboard**, and standard **error pages** ship. **Where the prototype differs from the shipped app (drawer-vs-page, layout), the prototype wins.** Components are reproduced with **real antd** — the prototype's framework-free recreations + `_ds_bundle.js` are reference only (per the design README). Post-v1 items (marketing, dark mode) + the polish passes (KPI honesty, visual-fidelity) stay in Week 7 / Post-v1 below. Each edit view is keyed on its route id so a param-only navigation reloads cleanly (review fix `ddbdc1d`). Version history stays the one read-only drawer (alongside Share). (Dedups [#204](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/204).) _(Phase 4.2, commit `95c7cd2`.)_

- [x] ✅ **Results drill-down → dedicated route** (`/results/:runId`, `RunDetail`) — promoted the in-page `openRun` drawer to a routed run-detail page with back-nav, stat cards (status / checks-passed / triggered-by / started / duration), per-check results table (severity tags + `metric_value` + observed), the per-check trend expandable row, and CSV/JSON download. Deep-linkable + refreshable (fetch by id); row click in the runs table navigates here (PR #333, Phase 1.1–1.2). _Design ref: `templates/app/ResultsScreen.jsx` → `RunDetailScreen`._

- [ ] ⬜ **Standard error pages (4xx / 5xx)** — an `ErrorState`-based page in the app shell switchable across 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 429 / 500 / 502 / 503 / 504; wire it into the router catch-all + the `ErrorBoundary` ([PR #210](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/210)) fallback so render-time + routing failures render in-brand. _Design ref: `templates/error/index.html` + `components/feedback/ErrorState.jsx` (+ `components/feedback/errorstate.card.html`); readme §"Templates → `templates/error/`"._

- [ ] ⬜ **New Connection → source-select + config (2-page flow)** — adopt the prototype's two-step New-Connection: a categorized **Select Source** page (**Orchestration first**, then Warehouses / Lakehouses / Cloud Storage) → the spec-driven **Configuration** page. Verify `ConnectionNew` (`/connections/new`) splits the source pick into its own step rather than one combined page. _Design ref: `templates/app/ConnectionFlow.jsx` (`ConnectionSourceScreen` → `ConnectionConfigScreen`); `connectionFormSpec.ts`; readme §"Templates" full-page-flows note._

content.text:
Phase 4 is complete. Here's the summary.

## Week 6 Phase 4 — Drawer → page restructures ✅

Branch `feature/w6-phase4-drawer-to-page`, 5 commits, all gates green locally (typecheck · lint · prettier · **259 tests** · production build).

**4.1 — New Suite page** ([1f437fa](#)) — extracted a shared `SuiteForm` (name/description/connection picker + run-target inputs) out of `SuiteDrawer`; [`/suites/new`](frontend/src/pages/SuiteNew.tsx) reuses it and, on create, continues to `/suites/:id/checks/new` so a suite is never left empty.

**4.2 — Edit flows → pages** ([95c7cd2](#)) — `/connections/:id/edit`, `/suites/:id/edit`, `/suites/:id/checks/:checkId/edit` as deep-linkable pages reusing the create forms (`ConnectionForm`/`SuiteForm` gained an edit mode). **Removed `ConnectionDrawer`, `SuiteDrawer`, `CheckDrawer`** + their tests; parents navigate instead of holding drawer state. Added `getCheck`. Version history stays the one read-only drawer alongside Share. Dedups #204.

**4.3 — Connection source-select split** ([94af936](#)) — categorized picker (Orchestration first → Warehouses/Lakehouses/Cloud Storage) with avatar + blurb + chevron cards; grouping is presentation-only in [connectionSources.ts](frontend/src/components/connections/connectionSources.ts), the load-bearing split still flows through `CONNECTION_KIND`.

**Review** — ran the multi-angle `/code-review`. It surfaced one real correctness bug: the edit pages fetched via `useAsyncData` keyed on an internal nonce, so a param-only navigation between two edit URLs would keep stale data and could PATCH the wrong id. Fixed ([ddbdc1d](#)) by keying each edit view on its route id (forces remount → refetch + reseed, with a regression test) and parallelized CheckEdit's `getConnection`. One deferred cleanup noted (3 edit pages share a load+header+Card shell → an `EditPageShell` wrapper, natural to fold in when Phase 6 adds the 404 branches).

Docs: `progress.md` + `WEEK6_TODO.md` ticked (Week 6 now **16/27**); epic #175 mirror synced.

Nothing is pushed — the branch is local. Want me to open the PR, or move on to Phase 5 (Profile / Settings / Admin reconcile)?

input.command:
cat > /tmp/pr_body_p4.md <<'EOF'
## Week 6 — Phase 4: drawer → page restructures

Adopts the prototype's dedicated-page model (ADR 0022 — **prototype wins on any drawer-vs-page conflict**). Every create/edit flow becomes a deep-linkable page; the **Share** drawer (and the read-only version-history drawer) are the only surviving drawers.

### 4.1 — New Suite page (`1f437fa`)
- Extracted a shared `SuiteForm` (name/description/connection picker + datasource-shaped run-target inputs) out of `SuiteDrawer`.
- `/suites/new` reuses it; on create it continues to `/suites/:id/checks/new` so a fresh suite is never left empty.
- `Suites.tsx` "New suite" → `navigate('/suites/new')`.

### 4.2 — Edit flows → pages (`95c7cd2`) — dedups #204
- `/connections/:id/edit` → `ConnectionEdit` (reuses `ConnectionForm`)
- `/suites/:id/edit` → `SuiteEdit` (reuses `SuiteForm`)
- `/suites/:id/checks/:checkId/edit` → `CheckEdit`
- **Removed `ConnectionDrawer`, `SuiteDrawer`, `CheckDrawer`** + their tests; parents drop `editing`/`drawer` state for `navigate()`.
- Create + edit share one form per entity (`ConnectionForm`/`SuiteForm` gained an edit mode; check form helpers were already shared). Added `getCheck`.
- Version history stays the one read-only drawer (alongside Share).

### 4.3 — New Connection source-select split (`94af936`)
- Categorized **Select Source** step — Orchestration first, then Warehouses / Lakehouses / Cloud Storage, with an Orchestration lead-in nudge; cards gain the type avatar + a one-line blurb + a chevron → spec-driven **Configuration** step.
- Grouping is presentation-only (`connectionSources.ts`); the load-bearing datasource-vs-orchestration split still flows through `CONNECTION_KIND` (CLAUDE.md §4).

### Review fix (`ddbdc1d`)
`/code-review` caught a real bug: the edit pages fetched via `useAsyncData` keyed on an internal nonce, so a param-only navigation between two edit URLs kept stale data and could PATCH the wrong id. Fixed by keying each edit view on its route id (remount → refetch + reseed; regression test added) and parallelized CheckEdit's `getConnection` fetch.

### Verification
Local gates green: `tsc --noEmit` · ESLint (0 warnings) · Prettier · **259 vitest** · production build (recharts stays its own lazy chunk).

### Deferred (noted, not filed)
The 3 edit pages share a load+header+Card shell that could fold into one `EditPageShell` wrapper — natural to do when Phase 6 adds the 4xx/5xx error branches.

Closes part of #175 (Week 6 → 16/27).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
gh pr create --base main --head feature/w6-phase4-drawer-to-page \
  --title "feat(frontend): Week 6 Phase 4 — drawer→page restructures (new-suite/edit pages + connection source-select)" \
  --body-file /tmp/pr_body_p4.md 2>&1 | tail -3
